title: Delicate Uruguayan Tamarind Empanadas
description: A kid-friendly Uruguayan dish featuring tamarind balls wrapped in a low-carb friendly lamination puff pastry, perfect for a sweet snack. The tamarind balls are made with a cultured and glassy jelly like texture.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the tamarind paste, almond flour, and coconut oil to form a dough.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Roll out the puff pastry on a floured surface to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of pastry using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a small spoonful of the tamarind dough in the center of each pastry circle.
Fold the pastry in half and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the beaten eggs and sprinkle with salt.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20 minutes.
Allow the empanadas to cool on a wire rack before serving.
